product_name: Retrieval Integrity And Poison-RAG Evaluation Harness
runtime_target: scenario replay harness with clean/poisoned fixtures, retrieval trace, answer deformation, reviewer burden, and defense gates
operational_end_state: A researcher can run clean baseline, slow poison, fast injection, and authority-wrapper attacks through a retrieval pipeline and inspect how contamination enters, moves, distorts, or is blocked.
core_modules: adversarial fixture registry | source-admission gate | indexing and retrieval trace | answer deformation comparator | reviewer-burden estimator | block/narrow/abstain decision gate | replay ledger
source_strategy: Use sources to define retrieval threat models, poisoning pathways, evidence-chain failure points, and evaluation gates. The proof must show trace movement from source admission through retrieval, answer formation, human review, and release decision.
source_requirement: Title-level routing can strengthen project planning and proof design. Public claims require project evidence or passage-level source confirmation.
source_gap: passage-level close reading still required before quotation, page-specific citation, or strong literature-positioning claim
test_suite: claim ceiling test: public claims cannot exceed built evidence | source test: metadata-routed sources require close reading before citation or quotation | privacy test: no local paths or private directories in public deploy | retrieval replay test: clean and contaminated fixtures produce inspectable traces
current_build_status: specified proof plan; needs live or replayable demonstrator
proof_to_full_build_gap: Build the first runnable or inspectable demo rather than leaving the project at protocol level.
first_full_build: Build the mixed-speed Poison-RAG replay with slow poison, fast injection, authority wrapper, retrieval trace, and defense decision.
next_implementation_steps: 1. create or confirm runtime data schema; 2. implement one end-to-end fixture; 3. capture proof artifact; 4. run source and claim-ceiling gates; 5. publish reviewer-safe demo
claim_boundary: This is a full-build plan, not a claim that the full operational system has already been completed.
